content: The original Self-Refine framework’s single-perspective iterative feedback loop can be enhanced by integrating multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective self-refine framework that improves output quality without adding training overhead or external supervision. This framework leverages disagreement between the LLM’s own divergent perspectives to catch flaws that a single self-feedback loop would miss, much like how a person catches their own mistakes by rethinking a problem from multiple angles rather than just tweaking their first idea.
